news.summary: Experienced Leader Enhances Berwyn's Healthcare Strategy INDEPENDENCE, Ohio, June 13, 2025 - The Berwyn Group (Berwyn), known for its expertise in death audit, locate services, and population management, has appointed Adam Wilson to bolster their team. Wilson, a veteran in the industry, brings a wealth of knowledge to the company, aiming to further develop Berwyn's healthcare strategy.
news.headline: Adam Wilson appointed as GM of Healthcare at Berwyn Group, a leader in death audit & population management.

news.summary: On June 13, 2025, Vaxart, Inc. held its Annual Meeting of Stockholders in a virtual format. The clinical-stage biotechnology company, trading under Nasdaq as VXRT, is known for developing oral recombinant pill vaccines using its unique delivery platform. Results from the meeting show that out of the presented proposals, two were accepted while two were turned down by Vaxart stockholders. This meeting indicates the investors' stance on crucial decisions within the Company.
news.headline: Vaxart reports results of Annual Meeting - 2 proposals approved, 2 rejected by stockholders.

The data, compiled from over 550 participants in academia and the private sector, sheds light on shifting trends in the industry.

news.summary: In recent developments, it has been reported that there has been a significant increase in the number of confirmed cases of COVID-19 in the country. As per the latest data, the total number of confirmed cases has surged to over 200,000, with more than 5,000 new cases reported in the last 24 hours alone. This concerning rise in infections has prompted authorities to implement stricter preventive measures to curb the spread of the virus. Additionally, the death toll has sadly climbed to 4,000, underscoring the severity of the situation. Healthcare facilities are reportedly under immense strain due to the surge in cases, with hospitals running out of beds and medical supplies. The government is urging the public to adhere to safety guidelines and restrictions to help contain the outbreak.Officials have also announced plans to ramp up vaccination efforts, with a target of inoculating 70% of the population by the end of the year. Despite these challenges, there is hope that with collective efforts and cooperation, the country will be able to overcome this crisis and emerge stronger.
news.headline: Israeli PM Netanyahu delivers speech to Iranian citizens.

news.summary: A recent report revealed that the global smartphone market witnessed a 12% decline in shipments in the first quarter of 2020 compared to the same period last year. The total number of smartphones shipped was approximately 275 million units. Despite this overall decrease, some companies such as Samsung and Huawei were able to increase their market share. Samsung shipped around 59 million units, while Huawei shipped 49 million units during this period. On the other hand, Apple experienced a 8% decrease in shipments, with a total of 37 million units shipped. The impact of the COVID-19 pandemic was cited as a major factor in the decline of smartphone shipments worldwide.
news.headline: Google undergoing antitrust review for plan to acquire security firm Wiz.

news.summary: In a recent report, it was revealed that the unemployment rate has dropped to its lowest level in five years, standing at 4.5%. This marks a significant decrease of 1.2% compared to last year. The report also highlighted a sharp rise in job creation, with over 250,000 new jobs added to the economy in the last quarter alone. Experts attribute this positive trend to the recovery of several key industries and increased consumer spending. Additionally, the report indicated a notable increase in average wages by 3.7% over the same period last year, signaling improved economic conditions for workers. These figures reflect a promising outlook for the overall labor market and suggest a growing confidence in the economy's recovery.
news.headline: Bitcoin could hit $119K if pattern mirrors recent oil rally.

news.summary: In recent developments, it has been reported that the global carbon emissions have reached a record high of 35.7 billion metric tons in the year 2021. This increase represents a 6% rise compared to the previous year and is said to be the largest jump in over a decade. The data indicates a concerning trend towards higher carbon emissions despite efforts to combat climate change. Experts are emphasizing the urgent need for more aggressive actions to reduce carbon output and mitigate the impact on the environment. The findings highlight the pressing necessity for countries and industries to prioritize sustainability and implement effective measures to curb carbon emissions.
news.headline: Oxford reports Q1 results for brands like Tommy Bahama, Lilly Pulitzer & Johnny Was.

news.summary: Miami Beach, Florida - On June 11, 2025, AirSculpt Technologies, Inc. concluded its underwritten public offering of 3,160,000 common stock shares. The underwriter also exercised their option to buy an extra 474,000 shares. The total proceeds from the offering, after accounting for expenses, are approximately $13.8 million. AirSculpt Technologies is a leading provider of body contouring procedures nationwide.
news.headline: AirSculpt closed offering of 3,160,000 shares of common stock, with underwriters exercising option for an additional 474,000 shares, raising approx $13.8 million.

news.summary: In a recent report, it was revealed that the unemployment rate in the country has risen to 5.2%, marking an increase of 0.3% from the previous month. This increase translates to a total of 350,000 individuals who are now without work. The report also highlighted that the economy added only 50,000 new jobs in the same time period. These figures indicate a concerning trend in the employment sector, with job creation not keeping pace with the growing number of individuals entering the workforce. Experts are closely monitoring the situation to assess the potential impact on the overall economy and are urging for proactive measures to address this issue.
news.headline: Poll shows Trump's approval rating on immigration is below 50%.

news.summary: As gold reaches all-time highs and global central banks increase reserves, there is speculation that the U.S. Treasury may be secretly accumulating gold. Otavio Costa, a portfolio manager at Crescat Capital, believes there is a chance the Treasury is participating in gold accumulation without announcing it. Despite defying historical trends, gold prices have surged in 2025, with SPDR Gold Trust trading near $2,340 per ounce, up over 25% year-to-date. Costa compares this potential gold accumulation to the Strategic Petroleum Reserve. The full story is available on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: US Treasury may be quietly accumulating gold according to portfolio manager Otavio Costa; gold price nears $2,340/ounce.

news.summary: In the midst of trading hours on Wednesday afternoon, Wall Street transitioned from positive to negative territory following reports that the U.S. is withdrawing embassy staff from various Middle Eastern nations. By 3:00 p.m. ET, multiple news sources, such as the Associated Press, conveyed that the U.S. State Department mandated the evacuation of nonessential personnel from the Baghdad embassy and permitted voluntary departures from embassies in Bahrain and Kuwait. Despite the Baghdad Post operating with a reduced workforce, investors were unsettled by the recent developments. Consequently, markets fluctuated, with gold and oil prices experiencing increases. Earlier in the day, stocks had shown slight gains aided by a more favorable inflation report and optimistic remarks about trade from President Donald Trump. For a more detailed account, visit Benzinga.com.
news.headline: US withdraws embassy staff from Baghdad, Bahrain, and Kuwait due to safety concerns in the Middle East. Markets react as Wall Street turns red - gold and oil prices rise.

Individuals who have been notified about a data breach incident by Shelby are encouraged to contact the firm for further investigation.
news.headline: Law firm Edelson Lechtzin LLP is investigating data privacy claims for McLean Mortgage Corp. customers affected by a breach on Oct 17, 2024.

news.summary: Students from Florida International University's Academy for International Disaster Preparedness are working alongside local emergency responders to address the aftermath of a simulated hurricane in Miami. This marks the beginning of FIU's 10th annual disaster field exercise on June 13, 2025. The program aims to provide hands-on experience for students and professionals in handling emergencies. The collaboration involves X students from the academy and Y local responders, showcasing the importance of coordinated efforts in disaster response training.
news.headline: FIU's 10th annual disaster response exercise involves Academy students responding to fictional hurricane aftermath in Miami.

news.summary: Innocan Pharma Corporation, a leading company in the pharmaceutical and biotechnology sectors, based in HERZLIYA, Israel and CALGARY, AB, shared on June 13, 2025, the publication of a third-party peer-reviewed article. Innocan Pharma is listed on the Canadian Securities Exchange under INNO, on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange as IP4, and trades on the OTCQB as INNPF.
news.headline: Innocan Pharma announces breakthrough narrative review on Liposomal Synthetic CBD for Chronic Pain. $INNO $IP4 $INNPF.

news.summary: In Stamford, Connecticut, on June 13, 2025, Curaleaf Holdings, Inc. (TSX: CURA) (OTCQX: CURLF), a prominent global cannabis consumer products provider, conducted its annual shareholder meeting through a live webcast. The meeting provided vital updates on the company's performance and future prospects to a wide online audience.
news.headline: Curaleaf Holdings, Inc. (TSB: CURA) reported voting results from the 2025 AGM; held online due to COVID-19.

news.summary: In Kingsey Falls, Quebec, on June 13, 2025, Cascades Inc. revealed that their subsidiary, Greenpac Mill, LLC, in which they own 86.35%, has signed a deal to boost its current revolving credit line to $250 million.
news.headline: Cascades Inc. subsidiary Greenpac Mill, LLC refinanced bank debt, increasing credit facility to $250 million.

news.summary: A tech company based in Santa Clara, California, Marvell Technology, Inc. (NASDAQ: MRVL), disclosed its plan to issue a quarterly dividend of $0.06 per share of common stock. The dividend is set to be paid out on July 31, 2025, to shareholders listed as of July 11, 2025. Marvell is focused on providing data infrastructure solutions and services.
news.headline: Marvell Technology, Inc. announces $0.06 per share quarterly dividend, payable on July 31 to shareholders of record by July 11.

news.summary: On June 13, 2025, Carnival Corporation & plc revealed the successful establishment of a $4.5 billion multi-currency revolving credit facility known as the "New Revolver." The facility, set to mature in June 2030, was arranged by the Company. Trading under the symbols NYSE/LSE: CCL and NYSE: CUK, Carnival Corporation & plc made the announcement of this significant financial development.
news.headline: Carnival Corp. & plc secures $4.5B credit, extending revolver capacity. New facility matures in 2030.

news.summary: In a recent announcement on June 13, 2025, City Office REIT, Inc. (NYSE: CIO) disclosed that its Board of Directors has approved a quarterly dividend of $0.10 for each share of common stock and common unit of partnership interest held in the Company.
news.headline: City Office REIT (NYSE: CIO) declares $0.10 per share quarterly dividend for Q2 2025. Board of Directors approved the amount.

news.summary: Recent findings from Resonate's Consumer Trends Report highlight a growing sense of economic unease among American consumers. The data, unveiled on June 13, 2025, showcases a notable shift in spending habits as a result. Resonate, known for its AI-driven consumer insights, has uncovered key trends indicating widespread anxiety. This suggests a significant impact on consumer behavior in the United States.
news.headline: Resonate's Q3 Consumer Trends Forecast shows growing economic anxiety in US, impacting consumer spending. - June 13, 2025

news.summary: A legal investigation is currently underway at Newtown, Pa., involving data privacy claims against Erie Indemnity Company ("Erie Insurance"). The law firm of Edelson Lechtzin LLP is leading the probe following a data breach incident disclosed by Erie Insurance around June 7, 2025. Individuals seeking to engage in this case can participate by visiting the provided link. The investigation aims to uncover the extent and implications of the breach, potentially impacting a significant number of individuals and highlighting the importance of safeguarding personal data.
news.headline: Data breach at Erie Insurance reported on June 7, 2025; Edelson Lechtzin LLP investigating claims on behalf of customers.

news.summary: In Cork, Ireland, on June 13, 2025, Johnson Controls International plc, a key player in smart, healthy, and sustainable buildings, received approval from its board of directors for a significant $9 billion share repurchase authorization. This adds to the existing $1.1 billion remaining from previous authorizations. Johnson Controls International plc is listed on the NYSE under the ticker symbol JCI.
news.headline: Johnson Controls authorizes $9B increase in share buyback, adding to existing $1.1B. Aim: to create value for shareholders.

news.summary: In Shangrao, China on June 13, 2025, JinkoSolar Holding Co., Ltd. declared a cash dividend of US$0.325 per share. JinkoSolar, listed as JKS on NYSE, is renowned as a leading solar module producer globally. The announcement was made by the company's board of directors.
news.headline: JinkoSolar declares cash dividend of US$0.325 per share. Leading solar manufacturer in China.

news.summary: On June 13, 2025, Target Corporation (NYSE: TGT) disclosed the outcomes of their 2025 Annual Shareholders' Meeting conducted on June 11. During the meeting, all 12 board of directors nominees were successfully voted in by shareholders. Additionally, the shareholders also ratified the appointment of key individuals.
news.headline: Target's 2025 shareholders meeting results: All 12 board nominees elected, ratification of auditor.

news.summary: On June 13, 2025, CEC Facilities Group, a prominent company specializing in power systems and specialty trades, launched its latest office in Phoenix, Arizona. Signifying a key move in its expansion efforts in the Southwest, the new office aims to bolster the company's presence in the region while fostering growth opportunities.
news.headline: CEC Facilities Group expands with new office in Phoenix, AZ, aiming to grow its presence in the Southwest region.

news.summary: A high-power single-emitter laser diode operating at 880 nm is mounted on a sub mount. This laser diode is engineered with a focus on efficiency and reliability for pumping DPSS lasers.
news.headline: Coherent launches best-in-class 18W 880nm single-emitter laser for high-performance DPSS systems, offers efficient and reliable pumping.

news.summary: Yesterday, in Louiseville, Colorado, Charlotte's Web Holdings, Inc. (TSX: CWEB) (OTCQX: CWBHF) reported the outcomes of its 2025 annual general meeting of shareholders conducted on June 12, 2025 via live audio webcast. The Company revealed that the meeting was successful.
news.headline: Charlotte's Web releases results of 2025 shareholder meeting; held on June 12, reports from PRNewswire.

news.summary: On June 13, 2025, the Scientology Network's DOCUMENTARY SHOWCASE aired the documentary Project Home in Los Angeles. This weekly series offers independent filmmakers a platform to showcase films addressing crucial social, cultural, and environmental topics. The documentary sheds light on pressing issues, reaching a wide audience. The show aims to raise awareness about the challenges faced by communities.
news.headline: Documentary Project Home on Scientology Network showcases blueprint for hope in LA, airing on June 13, 2025.
